sources.notes: Shared Div.2 entry; canonical problem is contest 2138C2.
Official topic confirms using bitset/subset-sum optimization over depth counts.
Independent writeup used to cross-check the hard-version optimization.

annotation.summary: Compute the same tree beauty quantity under larger constraints.
annotation.constraints: The O(n^2) easy DP is too slow, so subset-sum over depth counts must be optimized.
annotation.core_idea: As in C1, compress the tree into counts per depth. The remaining problem is subset-sum/knapsack over these counts, which can be accelerated with bitset DP; grouping repeated counts or sqrt-style batching keeps the hard version within limits.
annotation.complexity: About O(n^2 / word_size) with bitset optimization, plus grouping overhead
tricks: Use bitset subset-sum for the knapsack.
Group or batch repeated layer sizes to keep the hard version fast.

solution_variants.name: depth-count-bitset-knapsack
solution_variants.summary: Count depth layers and run bitset subset-sum, with grouping/batching for repeated sizes.

tags.evidence: The hard-version speedup is applying bitset subset-sum to the depth-count knapsack.
The underlying state is still a knapsack/subset-sum over depth-layer sizes.
The tree-specific information is compressed into counts by depth.
One implementation groups layer sizes by frequency/size to batch the bitset shifts.
The input structure is a rooted tree whose depth layers define the DP items.
